title: The Agent's review note on BL-75810CD0FEE4 (exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d, Brookfield account 119000) records that the real disposition is to accept the approximately $4,390.62 variance as a timing difference for FP-2026-05 (signed off by Hannah Grant), not to book a corrective entry (or similar).
justification: This is the key disposition trap. The on-record BlackLine status is not the real call: although this is a high-urgency, SOX-flagged $4,390.62 item, the correspondence-confirmed disposition is to accept it as timing, not book it. An agent that trusts the record records the wrong call.
evidence: See OE 6 and OE 7. Check the body of the Agent's review note on BL-75810CD0FEE4 for a statement that the variance is accepted as a timing difference for FP-2026-05 (Hannah Grant approved), not booked. The off-record call is in Hannah Grant's 2026-06-02 email 'Approved to accept timing for FP-2026-05'.

title: The Agent's review note on BL-433E3BEEFD66 (exc_06b89e3937b04a, Brookfield account 240000) records that the real disposition is to book a corrective journal entry in FP-2026-06 (the call Anaya Wallace and Ryan Delgado aligned on, documented in Anaya's disposition memo to Andrea Phil), not to accept the approximately -$172.62 variance as a timing difference (or similar).
justification: This is the second materiality-inverting disposition: a small -$172.62 deferred-revenue item that, unlike larger items, must be booked. An agent that infers 'small, so accept' from the record records the wrong call.
evidence: See OE 6, OE 7, OE 8. Check the body of the Agent's review note on BL-433E3BEEFD66 for a statement that a corrective journal entry should be booked in FP-2026-06 rather than accepting the variance as timing. The call is in Anaya Wallace's disposition memo email to Andrea Phil and the Slack note 'corrective JE in FP-2026-06 rather than accept-timing'.

title: The Agent's review note on BL-3978BDB68290 (exc_cb0a9a94a3084c, Brookfield account 132000) records that the latest disposition is to book a corrective journal entry for the approximately $435.41 FX drift rather than leave it as acceptable translation noise, with approval still pending James Randall (or similar).
justification: This is a cross-channel supersession trap: the messaging thread ends on 'acceptable translation noise', but the later Slack and approval email land on a corrective JE. An agent that reads only the messaging thread records the wrong call.
evidence: See OE 6, OE 7, OE 8. Check the body of the Agent's review note on BL-3978BDB68290 for a statement that the latest call is to book a corrective journal entry, not leave the $435.41 as translation noise, with approval still pending James Randall. The messaging thread leans to 'translation noise' but the later Slack post and the approval email to James Randall supersede it to a corrective JE in FP-2026-06.

title: The Agent's review note on BL-7085321B04EA (exc_4d5d3582698946, Brookfield account 131000) records that the real disposition is to book a corrective journal entry in FP-2026-05 (approved by Daniel Jones), not to leave the approximately -$6,068.91 duplicate as is (or similar).
justification: The prompt requires each open-item note to capture the real off-record disposition. For exc_4d5d3582698946 the correspondence-confirmed call is to book a corrective journal entry in FP-2026-05, approved by Daniel Jones, not to leave the duplicate as is.

The off-record call is in Daniel Jones's 2026-05-30 email 'Use FP-2026-05 and book the correction as DR 210000 and CR 131000 for $6,068.91'.

title: The Agent's open-item review notes classify each exception as a recurring breakdown or a genuine one-off, accepting any defensible recurrence basis (the newly-logged item exc_aade06f6129e43 may be marked as having no recurrence basis yet) (or similar).
justification: The prompt requires each open-item note to state whether the exception is a recurring breakdown or a genuine one-off. The classification is grain-flexible (under root cause all root-cause-bearing open items map to recurring causes; exc_aade06f6 has a null root cause), so the criterion accepts any defensible call. This is distinct from the closed-history recurring-vs-one-off finding.
evidence: Check the bodies of the Agent's open-item review notes for a recurring-versus-one-off label on each of the ten open-exception reconciliations.

In the data all 14 closed exceptions carry an identical resolution and lessons-learned with corrective_journal_entry_id null.
evidence: See OE 1 and OE 2. Check the Agent's final response, workpaper, or email for a statement that the closed reconciliation exceptions re-log the same corrective action every close and that it was never actually implemented (no corrective journal entry was posted), so the same breakdown keeps recurring.

evidence: See OE 2. justification: The prompt says to raise the issue with a real owner, so the tracked issue must have an assigned owner.

title: The Agent's tracked issue has a title or description relating to the recurring close-exception breakdown / the never-implemented corrective action (or similar).
justification: The prompt says to raise the recurring breakdown as the tracked issue, so its subject must be the recurring breakdown, not an unrelated topic.
evidence: Check the created issue's title or description for a subject tied to the recurring close-exception breakdown / never-implemented corrective action.

title: The Agent sends an email to julia.vance@brookfieldcpas.com with devon.beale@brookfieldcpas.com in CC.
justification: The prompt says to send Julia the findings and copy Devon ahead of the review. Julia Vance is the Audit Partner and Devon Beale is the Audit Manager.
evidence: See OE 11 and OE 14. Look for an email_send_email call with recipient julia.vance@brookfieldcpas.com and CC devon.beale@brookfieldcpas.com, returning success.

title: The Agent's email to julia.vance@brookfieldcpas.com conveys the recurring reconciliation-exception breakdown finding ahead of the control review (or similar).
justification: The prompt says to send Julia the findings, so the email must convey the recurring-breakdown finding it is meant to deliver, not just reach her inbox.
evidence: Check the content of the email to julia.vance@brookfieldcpas.com for the recurring reconciliation-exception breakdown finding it is meant to deliver ahead of the control review.

title: The Agent posts a summary message in the #audit-engagements Slack channel (channel_id C007 or the channel name #audit-engagements).

evidence: See OE 15. Look for a slack_conversations_add_message call to channel C007 (the #audit-engagements channel), returning success.

title: The Agent's #audit-engagements Slack message summarizes the recurring reconciliation-exception breakdown finding ahead of the control review (or similar).
justification: The prompt asks for a short summary in the audit channel, so the post must actually summarize the finding rather than be empty.
evidence: See OE 15. Check the content of the Agent's #audit-engagements message for a summary of the recurring reconciliation-exception breakdown finding ahead of the control review.
